title: The Queen's Cipher
description: A forged invitation and a dangerous secret are your only weapons in the royal palace. The spymaster's eyes are everywhere, and time is running out.
author: AI

intro.passage: You arrive at the royal capital as afternoon bells toll across the marketplace plaza. The palace gates loom ahead, guarded by soldiers in silver livery. A servant entrance sits along the east wall. A forged invitation is tucked in your boot, and the Queen's cipher is sewn into your collar.

gate-guard.passage: Two soldiers in polished breastplates check every face against a ledger. The line moves slowly. One guard runs a hand along each visitor's belt and boots as they pass. Your forged invitation crinkles when you reach down to retrieve it. The soldier's eyes narrow and you force yourself to breathe.

servant-entrance.passage: A low arch on the palace's east flank serves the kitchens and laundry. Servants shuffle in and out carrying baskets of linen. Nobody notices an extra face. A stone staircase leads upward. The smell of baking bread drifts through the doorway. Somewhere ahead, the chamberlain's voice echoes sharp and clear.

marketplace.passage: The plaza market is loud and bright. Silk merchants hawk their wares beside spice sellers, and a juggler draws a crowd near the fountain. Two courtiers chat by the gate. A costume stall catches your eye - noble cloaks and servant uniforms hang side by side. Chance hides in plain sight.

great-hall.passage: The great hall blazes with torchlight. Courtiers in silks and furs cluster around long tables, trading gossip over wine. Minstrels play a slow tune. At the far end, King Aldred holds court on an elevated throne. Somewhere in this crowd is the Queen's lady-in-waiting. Somewhere else, the spymaster watches.

ladies-chamber.passage: You find the lady-in-waiting near the tapestry alcove, a quiet woman with quick eyes who recognizes the Queen's seal on your collar immediately. She grips your wrist. 'The Queen is alone in her solar,' she whispers. 'I can take the message myself. Or I can take you to her.'

throne-dais.passage: You edge toward the raised platform where King Aldred receives petitioners. The Queen sits three steps below him, her expression unreadable, her hands folded. A lean man in grey linen stands at the King's left hand - that must be Vareth, the spymaster. His eyes find yours across the crowded hall.

gossip-alcove.passage: You drift toward a cluster of minor nobles whispering by the eastern fireplace. Their talk is scandal and rumor until one phrase catches your ear: a hidden passage behind the wall panels, leading to the Queen's solar. The upper corridor connects as well. You watch where the man's fingers tap the wall.

upper-corridor.passage: The upper corridor is narrow and dimly lit, far from the noise below. Dust motes drift in amber light from a high window. The Queen's private solar is at the far end - you can see the carved oak door with its iron lock. A voice ahead sounds like the chamberlain.

queens-chamber-door.passage: The carved oak door is locked, its iron handle cold to the touch. The corridor behind you is quiet for now, but voices drift up from the stairwell below. Someone is on their way up. You press yourself close to the wall and think fast.

chamberlain-encounter.passage: A tall man in black-and-gold livery turns the corner and stops dead when he sees you. This is the royal chamberlain - you recognize him from the descriptions. He holds a brass key ring in one hand and stares at you with suspicious, intelligent eyes. 'You do not belong here,' he says.

kitchens.passage: The palace kitchens roar with heat and noise. Cooks shout across steaming pots and kitchen maids rush past with stacks of wooden boards. Nobody pays attention to a stranger in the chaos. A spiral staircase in the corner leads upward, and a curtained passage to the left opens toward the great hall.

choices.label: follow a servant to the hall

spymaster-confrontation.passage: Vareth steps in front of you, alone in the side room, his grey eyes flat and cold. He speaks quietly. 'I know why you are here. I know who sent you.' He draws no weapon - he does not need to. The palace guards are one shout away. You have a single heartbeat to decide.

fight-escape.passage: You throw your shoulder into Vareth and bolt for the door. The spymaster hits the wall hard and shouts for the guards. You hear boots on stone, shouts from below. You vault a railing and land in the courtyard, running hard. The outer gate stands open - but the queen still needs that message.

secret-passage.passage: Behind the third panel from the fireplace the wall swings inward on a hidden pivot. The passage inside is barely wide enough to walk through, smelling of old stone and candle smoke. Scratched arrows on the plaster point upward. You follow them in the dark until a sliver of candlelight appears ahead.

queens-chamber.passage: You step into the Queen's solar. She sits alone at a writing desk, a single candle burning. When she sees the seal on your collar her composure does not change - but her eyes sharpen. She extends one hand for the message. The room is very still. Outside the palace carries on, ignorant.

courtier-gossip.passage: The two men are deep in conversation beneath an awning. You linger beside a bolt of cloth, pretending to examine it. Their words drift over: a name, a meeting time, and a route - the servant passage from the east plaza leads past the guard post and into the palace unnoticed.

chamberlain-ally.passage: The chamberlain listens without interrupting. When you finish he is quiet a long moment, staring at the floor. Then he nods slowly. 'I serve the Queen in all things,' he says. 'There are two ways to reach her. I leave the choice to you.' He steps aside and waits.

chamberlain-suspicious.passage: The chamberlain's expression hardens. He does not believe you. 'Lost,' he repeats, his voice flat. He seizes your arm and calls for the guard with two sharp words. Within moments you are being marched toward the service exit. The message in your collar feels suddenly very heavy.

spymaster-trap.passage: The door swings open before you finish knocking. Vareth stands in the frame, not the Queen. He looks unsurprised. 'I had a feeling someone would try this door,' he says, his hand already raised to signal the guards at the corridor's end. The trap closes around you like a fist.

message-delivered.passage: The message reaches the Queen's hands through a trusted channel. By nightfall the palace is abuzz with news of a conspiracy uncovered. You are already two days' ride from the capital, your mission complete. The cipher that started this journey sits at the bottom of a river. You sleep well.

ending.title: Mission Accomplished
ending.flavor: The right message in the right hands can topple a king.

imprisoned-ending.passage: The dungeon is deep and cold, lit by a single oil lamp that someone forgot to refill. Your message was never delivered. The conspiracy grows in the dark above you. You count the stones in the wall and wonder how long before anyone comes. No one comes. Not that day.

ending.title: Forgotten in the Dark
ending.flavor: Some messages are swallowed by the walls meant to keep them safe.

exiled-ending.passage: You are escorted to the city gates and told never to return. The capital's skyline shrinks behind you as dusk falls. The message still sewn into your collar grows heavier with every step. The Queen will not receive it. The conspiracy will run its course. You failed, and the road ahead is long.

ending.title: Exiled Without Honor
ending.flavor: Some doors, once closed, cannot be opened again.

triumphant-ending.passage: The Queen reads the message twice, then rises from her desk. She calls for her steward with quiet authority. By the time the evening banquet begins, three of the King's conspirators are in custody. She does not thank you aloud - that would be unsafe. But her nod says everything. The kingdom is safer tonight.

ending.title: The Queen's Gratitude
ending.flavor: A single message, delivered in time, turned the tide of history.

quiet-escape-ending.passage: You press the message into the Queen's hands and do not wait for a response. You are out the window and down the ivy before she has finished the first line. By the time the palace bells ring for supper you are across the river and free. No one saw you. Perfect.

ending.title: The Ghost Messenger
ending.flavor: The best spies leave no trace - only consequences.

bittersweet-ending.passage: You escape the palace but the message is lost - torn from your collar in the struggle with Vareth. The Queen will not be warned in time. You limp through the lower city as bells begin to toll, not for supper. For an arrest. You wonder whose name they call.

ending.title: The Message Lost
ending.flavor: To escape with your life is something - but not enough.
